0.9.1.title: SlickBot v0.9.1
0.9.1.summary: Adds the Suggestions module with anonymous submissions, public voting, staff review states, discussion threads, and tracked launcher panels.
notes: Added the SUGGESTIONS module under Community Systems.
Members can submit suggestions through /suggestion submit or through a public Suggestions panel button.
Suggestions always post to the configured suggestions text channel, no matter where the command or panel is used.
Each suggestion post includes upvote/downvote buttons and a disabled total-vote button, and the embed shows upvote/downvote percentages.
Users can switch between upvote and downvote or press the same vote button again to remove their vote.
Suggestions can be anonymous by default. Anonymous submissions hide the submitter publicly while Senior Moderator+ users can reveal the submitter with /suggestion review reveal.
Suggestion embeds use the submitter profile image for visible submissions and the default Discord avatar for anonymous submissions.
Staff can change suggestions to Pending, Planned, Accepted, Denied, or Implemented with /suggestion review status and add revision notes with /suggestion review add-details.
Status changes update the original suggestion embed color, staff response, and revision notes below the suggestion details.
Each suggestion automatically attempts to create a discussion thread under the suggestion post.
Added configurable suggestion categories with starter defaults: Server, Discord, Stream, Events, Bot, and Other.
The Suggestions panel can be posted, edited, and refreshed; it reposts at the bottom of the panel channel whenever a new suggestion is submitted so the button remains easy to find.
Added /faq resend-navigation to resend the FAQ Navigation helper panel from inside an FAQ forum post.
Added suggestion permission actions, logging events, setup/module status, help entries, diagnostics, and backward-compatible PostgreSQL migrations.

0.9.0.title: SlickBot v0.9.0
0.9.0.summary: Adds a forum-backed Knowledge Base / FAQ module with an auto-updating master index and moderator FAQ reply tools.
notes: Added the FAQ module under Community Systems for forum-backed Knowledge Base navigation.
Added /faq setup to configure a Discord forum channel and create SlickBot's master FAQ index post.
SlickBot keeps individual FAQ posts staff-created and manages only the master index post.
The master FAQ embed links to FAQ forum posts and groups them by assigned forum tags, listing multi-tag posts under each matching category.
The master FAQ post refreshes automatically when forum posts are created, updated, deleted, or retagged, and can be manually refreshed with /faq refresh.

New FAQ forum posts automatically receive a navigation embed with a Return to Starting Menu button linking back to the master FAQ post and a Get Support button linking to the configured ticket channel when one is saved.
Added /faq edit, /faq status, /faq panel, and /faq answer.
Added autocomplete for /faq answer question: from the configured forum posts.
Added the FAQ Reply message context command so staff can right-click a user message, enter FAQ search text in a modal, and have SlickBot reply directly to that message with the matching FAQ link.
FAQ replies direct users to the configured ticket channel when one is saved.
Added FAQ permission actions, logging events, setup/module status, help entries, diagnostics, and backward-compatible PostgreSQL migrations.

0.8.9.title: SlickBot v0.8.9
0.8.9.summary: Adds the Community Games module with Counting, Tic-Tac-Toe, Connect Four, board-game XP awards, and configurable counting reactions.
notes: Added the COMMUNITY_GAMES module under the Community setup category with a Games overview, focused game configuration pages, and return-to-setup navigation.
Added separate enable and disable commands for Counting, Tic-Tac-Toe, and Connect Four.

Added a persistent counting channel system with configurable starting/current numbers, record tracking, incorrect-entry resets, consecutive-turn prevention, accepted-message edit/delete protection, optional safe math expressions, invalid-message deletion, reset messages, milestones, and contribution statistics.
Non-counting text messages are ignored by default in the counting channel so normal conversation does not reset the game.
Accepted counting messages are tracked for the active sequence; edits and deletions reset the count by default and can be disabled separately in Counting setup.

Added counting ignored-role and ignored-user lists, staff reset/correction commands, a public contribution leaderboard, optional milestone XP, and a setting controlling whether accepted counting messages earn normal message XP.
Added button-based Tic-Tac-Toe challenges with accept/decline controls, turn validation, win/draw detection, automatic expiration, and persistent player statistics.
Added button-based Connect Four challenges with a persistent board, column controls, turn validation, win/draw detection, automatic expiration, and persistent player statistics.

Counting setup now supports configurable accepted-count and failed-count reaction emojis. The defaults use the green-check and no-entry-sign equivalents, custom emoji strings can be provided through /games counting setup, and the accepted-count reaction path now includes alias/custom-emoji fallback resolution.

Counting failures now send an embed that pings the member, shows what they sent, shows the expected number, displays the 🏆 all-time server record, and calls out NEW RECORD SET when the failed run had reached a new all-time record.
Tic-Tac-Toe and Connect Four now award configurable Leveling XP when a player wins. Draws award half of the configured win XP to both players.

Added tracked public Community Games launcher panels with /games panel post, /games panel edit, and /games panel refresh. Counting panel buttons link to the configured counting channel; Tic-Tac-Toe and Connect Four buttons open a user-selection flow and return a direct link to the posted challenge message.
Added backward-compatible win_xp, accepted_reaction_emoji, and failed_reaction_emoji migrations for Community Games configuration.
Added the community_game_panels table for tracked, editable public Games launcher panels.
Added Community Games permission actions, public play defaults, logging, setup/module status, help entries, diagnostics, and backward-compatible PostgreSQL migrations.

0.8.8.title: SlickBot v0.8.8
0.8.8.summary: Standardizes support workflow review states for tickets, reports, applications, and appeals.
notes: Report resolve and dismiss controls now open a required decision-reason modal before finalizing the report.
Report review embeds now keep original details, show a clear decision reason, include an Add Details control while open, and use consistent status colors.
Ticket control embeds now show explicit status, opened/closed metadata, close reason, and transcript status when a ticket is closed but remains visible.

Application and appeal review embeds now use consistent support workflow status labels, with pending/open items in orange, approvals/resolutions in green, denials in red, and closed/dismissed items muted.
Appeal denial DMs now use the same red denial styling as the review channel embed.
Added a reports.decision_reason migration for clearer final report decision tracking.
Added confirmed support-module reset commands for Tickets, Reports, Applications, and Appeals so admins can restart individual support module setup/testing data without using the full server reset.
Each support reset shows a private confirmation prompt with affected record counts before any data is cleared.
Application types now have a configurable per-question session timeout with a default of 3 minutes.
Application question DMs now show a live Discord timestamp indicating how long the applicant has left to answer.
Expired application sessions are automatically cancelled and the applicant receives a DM explaining that the application was cancelled because they did not respond in time.
Report panels now use a Discord user picker before the required report-details modal, so reporters can select the user being reported instead of typing usernames or IDs.
Report panel submissions now only require the What Happened field; target user and message/context are optional.
Application panels can now include all enabled application types in one multi-application dropdown panel when /application panel is run without a specific type or with type:all.
Added /application review-index to post a refreshed review index for pending, approved, denied, or all applications, with links to the review messages in the review channel.
Application review indexes are deleted and resent at the bottom of the configured review channel when new applications arrive, statuses change, or reviewers change the index filter.
Restored /report user so the user option is required, while report panel submissions still support general reports with no selected user.
Added /application close and /application reopen so staff can temporarily stop or resume submissions for an application type without deleting its setup.
Application panels now keep closed application types visible and show a not-accepting-submissions message if a user tries to start one.
Fixed a PostgreSQL parameter typing issue that caused the report Add Details modal to fail on Railway.
Removed deprecated fetchReply interaction response options to avoid Discord.js deprecation warnings during private auto-delete replies.
Application review index filter buttons now silently refresh the index without sending a separate hidden confirmation message.
Application review index embeds are now titled after the selected application, using {application name} - Review Filter, or All Server Applications - Review Filters for all-type indexes.
Report Add Details entries now use Discord timestamps so staff can see exactly when each note was added.
Added /report review-index to post a refreshed Server Reports - Review Filter index with Open, Dismissed, Resolved, and All filters and links to report review messages.
Fixed Report Review Index filter buttons so they are handled as button interactions instead of select-menu interactions.
Report review indexes are deleted and resent at the bottom of the review channel when new reports arrive, report statuses change, or reviewers change the index filter.
Application question DMs now separate the question into a dedicated embed field so the prompt is easier for applicants to read.
Added /ticket review-index to post a Server Tickets - Review Filter index with Open, Unclaimed, Claimed, Escalated, Closed, and All filters.
Ticket review indexes link to active ticket channels/control messages and automatically refresh when tickets are opened, claimed, reprioritized, escalated, or closed.
Added /appeal review-index to post a Server Appeals - Review Filter index with Pending, Approved, Denied, and All filters and links to appeal review messages.
Appeal review indexes automatically refresh when appeals are submitted or approved/denied.
Ticket and appeal review indexes use the same delete-and-resend behavior as report/application indexes so the current index remains at the bottom of the selected staff channel.
Added backward-compatible ticket_review_indexes and appeal_review_indexes tables, support reset coverage, permission defaults, diagnostics, and setup/help references.

0.8.7.title: SlickBot v0.8.7
0.8.7.summary: Audits and tightens permissions for public panels, message triggers, native reactions, and interactive controls.
notes: Updated public ticket, report, application, appeal, birthday, role-panel, giveaway, and temp-voice controls so they honor matching public action permissions and module target restrictions.
Custom command chat triggers now check SlickBot Custom Commands public action access before responding in text channels.
Native reaction-role add/remove events now check the same public role-panel access rules as button/dropdown role panels, and unauthorized reaction adds are removed when possible.
Temporary voice owner controls still validate room ownership or staff authority, and now also respect the public temp-voice control action if admins restrict it.
Standardized component access-denied messages with a clear no-entry style private warning.
Removed duplicated permission default entries discovered during the audit.

0.8.6.title: SlickBot v0.8.6
0.8.6.summary: Refines setup-accessed panels, logging/status controls, permission team visibility, and moderation setup guidance.
notes: Removed batched/queued logging controls from the logging setup UI and command surface so configured logs are treated as instant delivery.
Added activity-type quick buttons and a Back to Setup button to the Status Control panel.
Added a saved stream URL setting through /status stream-url so the Status Control panel can switch to Streaming from a button.
Expanded Permission Teams and Permission Center panels with role/user membership details, team permission levels, explicit module grants, and explicit command grants.
Added a permission-team selector to the Permission Center so admins can inspect individual teams without leaving the panel.
Standardized setup-accessed panel headers with a master setup category and a clear Viewing label for the current subgroup.
Improved the Moderation module status and panel so it shows configured moderation items, missing logging setup, recent case totals, and recommended setup commands.
Removed member-facing submission/opening buttons from Support Center setup panels so ticket/report/application/appeal manager pages remain staff-control only.
Standardized the Tickets navigation button color inside the Support Center overview.
Status Control now displays the saved stream URL directly in the panel.
Added an Activity Text button and modal so admins can update the saved bot activity wording from the control panel.
Status and activity buttons now turn green when active and stay gray when inactive.

0.8.5.title: SlickBot v0.8.5
0.8.5.summary: Refines the setup center and module manager with consistent health states, detailed module setup guidance, and clearer next actions.
notes: Updated /setup to show setup categories, shared module health counts, and prioritized next actions.
Updated /modules panel with the same Ready, Partially Configured, Needs Setup, Disabled, Warning, Error, and Coming Soon status language used by /bot test.
Added module detail pages from the module manager so admins can review each module's purpose, current status, recommended setup steps, and useful commands.
Kept existing command names and added no full setup wizard yet, preserving current workflows while preparing for a future wizard pass.
Server Stats now treats any configured counter as ready because not every server needs all available counters.
Expanded the Community Center summary to include Custom Commands and Join-to-Create Voice, with buttons to open their focused panels.

0.8.4.title: SlickBot v0.8.4
0.8.4.summary: Adds an interactive /help menu and improves bot diagnostics with clearer module health, errors, and recommended fixes.
notes: Added /help as an interactive help center with enabled modules shown by default.
Help pages are filtered by the user's SlickBot permission level and module state so members only see commands relevant to them.
Added a Disabled Modules view inside /help for admins who want to inspect commands hidden by disabled modules.
Expanded /bot test with clearer Ready, Partially Configured, Needs Setup, Disabled, Warning, and Error statuses.
Updated diagnostic errors to use the Discord no_entry-style status icon and added recommended fixes for failed or incomplete module checks.
No command groups were renamed in this release.

0.8.3.title: SlickBot v0.8.3
0.8.3.summary: Improves Join-to-Create user controls with native Discord user select menus.
notes: Replaced typed-user modals for Permit User, Remove User, and Transfer Ownership with Discord user select menus.
Members can now search and select users through Discord's native picker instead of typing full usernames, mentions, or IDs.
Rename, Set Limit, and Delete Channel confirmation continue using modals because those controls need typed values.
Temporary voice control panels still refresh after selected-user actions so access and ownership changes stay current.

0.8.2.title: SlickBot v0.8.2
0.8.2.summary: Polishes Join-to-Create voice control panels with lock status emojis and modal-based owner controls.
notes: Added lock and unlock emojis to the Join-to-Create control panel buttons.
Updated the temporary voice status field to show 🔒 Locked or 🔓 Unlocked.
Added button-driven modals for Rename, Set Limit, Permit User, Remove User, and Transfer Ownership so members can adjust rooms without memorizing slash commands.
Changed Delete Channel from an immediate action to a confirmation modal that requires typing DELETE.
Control panels continue to refresh after owner actions so status, owner, and limit details stay current.

0.8.1.title: SlickBot v0.8.1
0.8.1.summary: Adds automatic temporary voice control panels with owner pings and quick buttons for Join-to-Create rooms.
notes: Temporary voice channels now receive an automatic control panel message in the voice channel chat when created.
The control panel pings the temporary channel owner so they can quickly find the controls.
Added quick buttons for Lock, Unlock, Claim, and Delete Channel.
The control panel shows owner, lock status, user limit, available slash commands, and auto-cleanup behavior.
The control panel updates after owner actions such as rename, limit changes, locking, unlocking, ownership transfer, and claiming.
SlickBot records control panel message IDs and logs a clear error when it cannot post the panel due to channel or permission limitations.

0.8.0.title: SlickBot v0.8.0
0.8.0.summary: Adds the Join-to-Create Voice module for temporary voice rooms with owner controls and automatic cleanup.
notes: Added the Join-to-Create Voice module as a completed implemented module.
Staff can register an existing voice channel as a join-to-create hub or create a new hub channel through Discord commands.
When a member joins a configured hub, SlickBot creates a temporary voice channel, moves the member into it, and tracks ownership in PostgreSQL.
Temporary voice channels support owner controls for rename, user limit, lock, unlock, permit, remove, transfer, and claim.
Empty temporary voice channels are automatically cleaned up after the configured delay, with startup repair for tracked channels after restarts.
Added join-to-create database storage, diagnostics, module status, permissions, and logging events.

0.7.0.title: SlickBot v0.7.0
0.7.0.summary: Adds the Custom Commands module for staff-managed text and embed responses triggered by members in chat.
notes: Added the Custom Commands module as a completed implemented module.
Staff can create, edit, delete, enable, disable, view, list, and test custom commands through Discord slash commands.
Members can trigger enabled custom commands with the configured prefix, such as !rules.
Custom command responses support plain text or embed mode, including optional embed title and color.
Added per-command cooldowns plus optional channel and role restrictions.
Added custom command database storage, usage tracking, diagnostics, module status, permissions, and logging events.

0.6.7.title: SlickBot v0.6.7
0.6.7.summary: Refines server stats so voice counters update from voice events while the interval refresh remains a backup.
notes: Updated voice-state handling so joining, leaving, or moving voice channels queues a near-immediate voice counter refresh.
Added a separate follow-up verification pass after voice changes so configured counters can be checked without delaying the voice counter.
Changed the periodic server stats fallback refresh from 5 minutes back to 15 minutes.
Improved voice counting by comparing voice-state cache and voice-channel member cache for a more reliable in-voice total.

0.6.6.title: SlickBot v0.6.6
0.6.6.summary: Adds the Bot Updates module and improves server stats reliability for member and voice counters.
notes: Added the Bot Updates module for configured release announcements with patch notes.
Added update-channel setup and optional role pings for bot update announcements.
Added preview and manual send tools for bot update messages.
Improved server stats refresh behavior with debounced event updates and clearer rename failure reporting.
Reduced unnecessary full member fetches during voice-state updates so voice counters can update more consistently.
